title: gilia millefoliata
intro: Gilia millefoliata, also known as yarrow gilia or bird's bill, is a perennial plant native to the western United States and Mexico, characterized by its white or pink flowers with a distinctive shape resembling a bird's bill. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. Gilia millefoliata is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating wounds. Herbal preparations of Gilia millefoliata include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
